mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP consistently predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as rising aggregate demand improves corporate earnings expectations and reduces equity risk premiums.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 9.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 9.0 percent, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate synchronized supply-side expansion and demand recovery, compressing discount rates and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or statistically insignificant Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns do not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the cost of capital and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days when the tightening cycle is active.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of observations during rate hike periods, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in retail and food services sales directly measures consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth months yield negative or flat S&P 500 20-day returns in over 50% of cases, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5 percent triggers inflation hedging outflows and higher discount rate expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 55% of months where CPI exceeds the 0.5 percent threshold, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC is greater than 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts reflects broad-based economic recovery, reinforcing earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce a positive Rank IC above 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window, or forward returns do not significantly outperform the baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index signal supply chain bottlenecks or capacity constraints, which increase input costs and compress profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where INDPRO contracts, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC exceeds 0.04.
